sections.heading: Federated Authentication
sections.body:
Link your IdP (Identity Provider) to Atlas if you provide each with the
appropriate metadata. After you link your IdP (Identity Provider) to Atlas, you can
map domains, organizations, and roles to your IdP (Identity Provider). To learn more, see
[Configure Federated Authentication](atlas-federated-authentication).

sections.heading: Multi-Factor Authentication
sections.body:
Configure MFA (multi-factor authentication) to help you control access to your Atlas accounts.
To set up MFA (multi-factor authentication), see [enable multi-factor authentication](atlas-enable-mfa).

sections.heading: Atlas Backend Infrastructure Access Restrictions
sections.body:
Allow temporary access for MongoDB Support to your Atlas infrastructure,
including [granting and revoking access](restrict-access) at
the cluster level.

sections.heading: Atlas CLI Authentication
sections.body:
Use Atlas CLI for secure authenticated access to your deployment
via [Atlas CLI Authentication](atlas-ui-authentication-streams)
to manage your MongoDB Atlas deployments. Set up access using Atlas
credentials or an API key.
